topic: iran us tensions escalate with drone strike and retaliation fears
headline_marked: [[US launches new attacks on Iran]] after Trump's threat, Iran closes Strait of Hormuz
headline: US launches new attacks on Iran after Trump's threat, Iran closes Strait of Hormuz
summary: Explosions were heard in several Iranian cities, and air defense systems were activated in Tehran's western areas. The US Central Command confirmed the defensive operations, citing Iran's 'unprovoked and continued aggression'.
details: The United States has launched new attacks on Iran, with explosions reported in several Iranian cities, including Bandar Abbas, following President Donald Trump's threat of a 'hard' strike. Air defense systems were activated in Tehran's western areas.

In response, Iran has once again closed the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian news agency Mehr, citing military sources, reported that the military has ordered a halt to all vessel traffic, including oil tankers and commercial ships, through the strait, warning that any violation would be targeted.

The US military's Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed the new attacks in Iran, stating that simultaneous defensive operations were initiated against several Iranian targets. CENTCOM indicated these strikes were in response to Iran's 'unprovoked and continued aggression', without specifying exact locations.

Iranian media reported significant explosions in the port city of Bandar Abbas, as well as on Qeshm Island and in the southern cities of Gorgan, Sirik, and Minab. Authorities in Tehran's western region activated air defense systems.

President Trump had earlier declared his intention to strike Iran 'hard' again, making the announcement on Wednesday after both sides exchanged attacks on Tuesday night. Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump stated, 'We hit them hard yesterday, and we're going to hit them hard today'.

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth echoed Trump's warning, saying on Wednesday that the US Central Command would be 'very busy tonight'. In response, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian posted on X that Iran would stand firmly against any pressure or threats.

The conflict between the US and Iran intensified on Tuesday after a US military helicopter was downed in the Strait of Hormuz. The US retaliated with airstrikes on various Iranian targets, to which Iran responded with missile and drone attacks on US bases in Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain.

Following the exchange of attacks, President Trump accused Iran of delaying negotiations and warned they would 'pay a price'. Conversely, Iran accused the US of repeatedly violating ceasefire agreements and stated it would re-evaluate its 'diplomatic communication' with Washington.

Amidst this renewed tension, diplomatic efforts to bring Washington and Tehran back to the negotiating table are ongoing. As part of these efforts, a Qatari delegation visited the Iranian capital, Tehran, on Wednesday.
caption:
The US has launched new attacks on Iran, prompting Iran to close the Strait of Hormuz. Explosions rocked several Iranian cities following President Trump's 'hard' attack threat.

The United States has launched new attacks on Iran, with explosions reported in several Iranian cities, including Bandar Abbas, following President Donald Trump's threat of a 'hard' strike. Air defense systems were activated in Tehran's western areas.

In response, Iran has once again closed the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian news agency Mehr, citing military sources, reported that the military has ordered a halt to all vessel traffic, including oil tankers and commercial ships, through the strait, warning that any violation would be targeted.

The US military's Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed the new attacks in Iran, stating that simultaneous defensive operations were initiated against several Iranian targets. CENTCOM indicated these strikes were in response to Iran's 'unprovoked and continued aggression', without specifying exact locations.

Iranian media reported significant explosions in the port city of Bandar Abbas, as well as on Qeshm Island and in the southern cities of Gorgan, Sirik, and Minab. Authorities in Tehran's western region activated air defense systems.

President Trump had earlier declared his intention to strike Iran 'hard' again, making the announcement on Wednesday after both sides exchanged attacks on Tuesday night. Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump stated, 'We hit them hard yesterday, and we're going to hit them hard today'.

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth echoed Trump's warning, saying on Wednesday that the US Central Command would be 'very busy tonight'. In response, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian posted on X that Iran would stand firmly against any pressure or threats.

Source: Prothom Alo, Somoy News

topic: fifa world cup fever sweeps bangladesh
headline_marked: [[Brazil players give coach Ancelotti birthday guard of honour]] at World Cup training, joined by Spike Lee
headline: Brazil players give coach Ancelotti birthday guard of honour at World Cup training, joined by Spike Lee
summary: Brazil's squad celebrated coach Carlo Ancelotti's 67th birthday with a guard of honour at their pre-World Cup training session in New Jersey, where filmmaker Spike Lee also made a surprise appearance.
details: Brazil's national football team players honored coach Carlo Ancelotti with a guard of honour for his 67th birthday during a pre-World Cup training session. The celebration took place at the New York Red Bulls training facility in suburban New Jersey.

The entire squad, except for the injured Neymar, welcomed a smiling Ancelotti onto the pitch. This festive moment occurred just three days before the Italian coach leads the record five-time World Cup winners into their opening match.

Veteran filmmaker Spike Lee, 69, also attended the session as a surprise spectator, sporting a Brazil top and cap. Lee expressed his strong support, stating, "I want Brazil to win," and referred to Neymar as "my man."

Brazil's all-time record scorer, Neymar, has been sidelined since 2023 due to injuries. He is currently recovering from a calf problem and is struggling to regain fitness for the start of the World Cup tournament.
Despite his enthusiasm for Brazil, Lee's visit was brief. He planned to return to Manhattan later that day to attend the fourth game of the NBA finals, where his beloved New York Knicks were playing.
caption:
Brazil's national team gave coach Carlo Ancelotti a special birthday surprise! The squad formed a guard of honour for his 67th birthday during a World Cup training session.

Brazil's national football team players honored coach Carlo Ancelotti with a guard of honour for his 67th birthday during a pre-World Cup training session. The celebration took place at the New York Red Bulls training facility in suburban New Jersey.

The entire squad, except for the injured Neymar, welcomed a smiling Ancelotti onto the pitch. This festive moment occurred just three days before the Italian coach leads the record five-time World Cup winners into their opening match.

Veteran filmmaker Spike Lee, 69, also attended the session as a surprise spectator, sporting a Brazil top and cap. Lee expressed his strong support, stating, "I want Brazil to win," and referred to Neymar as "my man."

Brazil's all-time record scorer, Neymar, has been sidelined since 2023 due to injuries. He is currently recovering from a calf problem and is struggling to regain fitness for the start of the World Cup tournament.

Despite his enthusiasm for Brazil, Lee's visit was brief. He planned to return to Manhattan later that day to attend the fourth game of the NBA finals, where his beloved New York Knicks were playing.

#Brazil #WorldCup #Football #Ancelotti #SpikeLee #SportsNews

Source: Prothom Alo, Somoy News, The Daily Star
